---
format: 
  html:
    link-external-icon: true
    link-external-newwindow: true
from: markdown+autolink_bare_uris
number-depth: 2
toc-depth: 2
date: last-modified
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY -- HH:mm:ss (z)"
---
# Welcome to the OUCRU introduction to Medical Statistics 2026 {.unnumbered}

- [Time table](handouts/Agenda_StatCourse_23-27March2026.pdf)
- The training will be **09:15 – 16:45 (UTC +7)** for everyday
- There will be a **lunch break between 12:30 – 13:30 (UTC +7)**
- Lunch will be provided for in-person attendees

## Contributors {.unnumbered}

- Prof. Ronald Geskus, Biostatistics group, OUCRU HCM
- Dr. Duc Du Hong, Biostatistics group, OUCRU HCM
- Dr. Hoang Van Thuan, Biostatistics group, OUCRU HCM
- Dr. Nguyen Lam Vuong, Biostatistics group, OUCRU HCM
- Dr. Tran Thai Hung, Biostatistics group, OUCRU HCM


# Pre-course setups and requirements

You can do the exercises directly using the web-R versions without any additional setup.
If you would like to do the exercises using RStudio, please follow [this handout](handouts/precourse_setup.qmd){target="_blank"} before joining the course.

# Course topics

## Day 1: Descriptive and exploratory data analysis 

### 🧑‍🏫️ Lead instructors: Ronald and Duc

### Morning: Descriptive data analysis

- [Lecture slides](slides/day_1/1.SlidesDescriptives.pdf)
- Contents:
  - 1. Data characteristics
  - 2. Variable types
  - 3. Variable summaries: numerical and graphical
  - 4. Introduction to R and RStudio

- Exercises:
  - [Webr version](handouts/day_1/Exercise1_Webr.html){target="_blank"}
  - [R studio version](handouts/day_1/ExerciseRStudio.html){target="_blank"}; [Exercise script (download)](handouts/day_1/ExerciseCode.R){target="_blank"}
- Answers: 
  - [R studio](handouts/day_1/ExerciseAnswers.html){target="_blank"}; [pdf](handouts/day_1/ExerciseAnswers.pdf){target="_blank"}

### Afternoon: Exploratory data analysis

- [Lecture slides](slides/day_1/2.SlidesEDA.pdf)
- Contents:
  - 1. (Baseline) tables
  - 2. Graphs: ≥ 2 categorical variables
  - 3. Graphs: numeric variables by group
  - 4. Graphs: 2 numeric variables

- Exercises:
  - [Webr version](handouts/day_1/ExerciseWebr-2.html){target="_blank"}
  - [R studio version](handouts/day_1/ExerciseRStudio-2.html){target="_blank"}; [Exercise script (download)](handouts/day_1/ExerciseCode-2.R){target="_blank"}
- Answers: 
  - [webr](handouts/day_1/ExerciseWebrAnswers-2.html){target="_blank"}; [R studio](handouts/day_1/ExerciseAnswers-2.html){target="_blank"}
